Default to Chinese prose, but preserve English paper titles, venue names, model names, tool names, and links exactly.

For monthly paper reports, keep context lean. Do not proactively read repository documentation such as README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/paper-tracking-sop.md, or docs/research-topics.md unless the user explicitly asks or it is necessary to avoid duplicate edits in target files.

Create or update a single file under docs/monthly named by the investigated month in YYYY-MM.md, not by the execution date. High-signal papers should also be synced into docs/papers.md when the task asks for it.

Prefer quality over volume. Use title screening first, then abstract close reading. Do not include a paper if its relevance cannot be defended from the abstract and public evidence.

When editing docs/papers.md, avoid duplicate entries, place papers in the most suitable existing section, keep summaries concise, and follow the current recommendation-line style. Do not invent multiple reviewers when only one agent is writing.

Community signal such as Hugging Face Papers reactions, alphaXiv activity, or GitHub stars should be used as supporting evidence only. Popularity cannot override weak topical fit or weak empirical substance.
